sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Price-Driven Insight from (AEFC) for Rule-Based Strategy
feed.summary: This article provides a price-driven insight for Aegon Funding Company Llc (NASDAQ: AEFC), highlighting a full alignment in neutral sentiment that favors a wait-and-see approach. It details exceptional risk-reward short setup and offers three institutional trading strategies tailored to different risk profiles. The analysis includes multi-timeframe signal analysis and AI-generated signals for informed decision-making.

feed.title: Enterprises Ready To Scale AI Beyond Pilots, Says Wipro's Rishad Premji
feed.summary: Wipro Executive Chairman Rishad Premji believes 2026 will be a pivotal year for AI adoption, with companies moving beyond pilot projects to large-scale implementation. He emphasized the importance of strong data foundations and contextual depth for large language models. Premji also highlighted India's potential to build multilingual AI models and become a global leader in AI deployment at scale, leveraging its digital public infrastructure and engineering talent.

feed.title: OMFL ETF: Contrarian Factor Mix, Remains A Buy (BATS:OMFL)
feed.summary: The Invesco Russell 1000 Dynamic Multifactor ETF (OMFL) is a contrarian investment vehicle with a macro-centered multifactor strategy designed to recalibrate the Russell 1000. Despite recent underperformance against the S&P 500, the ETF's current portfolio offers a margin of safety against potential market shifts. The author maintains a "Buy" rating, citing the strategy's past success in navigating market changes and continued optimism about its unique approach.

feed.title: Stride, Jabil, Benchmark, Knowles, and Connection Shares Are Soaring, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Shares of Stride, Jabil, Benchmark, Knowles, and Connection surged after President Trump called off tariffs on European allies, cooling fears of a transatlantic trade war. This news led to a broad market rally, with technology stocks like Nvidia and AMD leading the recovery, and the Nasdaq Composite and S&P 500 reversing recent losses. The market's positive reaction was also supported by a stabilization in bond yields, creating a more favorable environment for equity valuations.

feed.title: Goldman Sachs issues $16 billion in new notes with maturities through 2047
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs has announced the issuance of $16 billion in new debt securities with maturities ranging from 2029 to 2047, according to a press release and SEC filing. These new offerings include various fixed/floating rate and floating rate notes. This move follows recent analyst price target increases for Goldman Sachs and ongoing leadership changes within its global credit business.

feed.title: Regions Financial Corp (RF.PR.C) Declares Consistent Quarterly Dividend
feed.summary: Regions Financial Corp's preferred stock (RF.PR.C) is maintaining its quarterly dividend of $0.3562 per share, equating to a 5.78% forward yield. Despite a recent stock decline following Q4 results, the company projects net interest income growth of 2.5% to 4% for 2026 and demonstrates strong financial health with a Piotroski F-Score of 9. However, valuation metrics suggest the stock may be modestly overvalued, and its dividend yield is near a 2-year low.

feed.title: Insider Sell: Andrew D'Amico Sells 200 Shares of Vicor Corp (VICR)
feed.summary: Andrew D'Amico, a Director at Vicor Corp (VICR), sold 200 shares of the company on January 20, 2026, and now owns no shares. Over the past year, D'Amico has sold 37,588 shares with no purchases, contributing to a total of 45 insider sells for Vicor Corp. The stock, valued at $155 per share on the day of the sale, is considered significantly overvalued with a price-to-GF-Value ratio of 2.82.

feed.title: Merck & Co., Inc. (NYSE:MRK) Stock Price Up 1.5% on Analyst Upgrade
feed.summary: Merck & Co., Inc. (NYSE:MRK) saw its stock price increase by 1.5% after TD Cowen raised its price target to $120, though maintaining a "hold" rating. This upgrade, along with positive clinical data for its personalized mRNA vaccine and a partnership for an Ebola vaccine, contributed to improved analyst sentiment despite a recent earnings per share miss. The company also raised its quarterly dividend to $0.85, offering an annualized yield of approximately 3.1%.

feed.title: International Business Machines (NYSE:IBM) Shares Up 2.1% Following Analyst Upgrade
feed.summary: International Business Machines (IBM) shares rose 2.1% after JPMorgan Chase & Co. increased its price target from $290 to $312, maintaining a "neutral" rating. The stock currently holds a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating among analysts, with a target of $306.86. IBM is increasingly being viewed as an AI growth company, driven by its watsonx platform and new partnerships, with its upcoming Q4 earnings on January 28th being a significant catalyst.

feed.title: Regions taps Global Payments' Worldpay to enhance business payment services
feed.summary: Regions Bank has partnered with Worldpay, a Global Payments company, to provide its business banking customers with advanced payment tools and real-time data insights. This collaboration aims to enhance payment services for businesses banking with Regions.

feed.title: Dexcom Reports 22% Revenue Growth to $1.2 Billion in 2024 Amidst Challenges
feed.summary: Dexcom reported strong Q3 2024 revenue growth of 22% year-over-year, reaching $1.2 billion, despite a Class 1 recall for some G7 receivers. The company expanded its customer base to nearly 2.9 million and sees significant potential in the 4.5 million diabetes patients yet to adopt CGM technology, especially with the introduction of its new Stelo product for non-insulin users. Wall Street analysts maintain a positive outlook for DXCM, with an average 1-year price target of $84.81.

feed.title: Royal Caribbean Group Named to Fortune’s World’s Most Admired Companies List for 2026: What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Royal Caribbean Group has been included in Fortune’s World’s Most Admired Companies 2026 list, recognizing its leadership in the global vacation industry. This achievement highlights the company's commitment to innovation, excellent guest experiences, and strategic growth across its brands, including Royal Caribbean, Celebrity Cruises, and Silversea. The recognition also underscores Royal Caribbean Group’s dedication to setting new standards through initiatives like its Icon Class ships, expanding private destinations, and innovative loyalty programs.

feed.title: Starbucks Settles COBRA Suit From Ex-Worker's Spouse
feed.summary: Starbucks has reached a settlement in a proposed class action lawsuit concerning alleged failures in its post-employment medical insurance notices. The suit, filed in Florida federal court, involved employee health plan participants and beneficiaries who claimed the coffee retailer fell short on its COBRA obligations.

feed.title: Brown & Brown Keeps Quarterly Dividend at $0.165 a Share, Payable Feb. 11 to Holders of Record Feb. 4
feed.summary: Brown & Brown, Inc. announced that it will keep its quarterly cash dividend at $0.165 per share. The dividend is payable on February 11 to shareholders of record as of February 4. This ensures a consistent payout for investors in the multiline insurance and brokers group.

feed.title: With A 7% Dividend Yield And Growth Strategy, Verizon’s Outlook Takes Center Stage (VZ)
feed.summary: Verizon Communications Inc. (VZ) offers a 7%+ dividend yield backed by over $20B in annual free cash flow, suggesting dividend security despite a $150B debt load. The company is undergoing a CEO transition with the appointment of PayPal veteran David Schulman, who is expected to implement a new customer-centric strategy. Investor confidence hinges on the upcoming earnings report and 2026 guidance, especially given muted analyst expectations and an unchanged 2025 outlook.

feed.title: Lobbying Update: $2,710,000 of SOUTHERN COMPANY lobbying was just disclosed
feed.summary: SOUTHERN COMPANY (SO) recently disclosed $2,710,000 in lobbying expenditures for Q4 2025, covering a broad range of issues including climate change, environmental regulation, energy efficiency, and infrastructure. The article also provides data on SO stock trading by members of Congress and company insiders, as well as institutional investor activity and analyst ratings with price targets.

feed.title: Brown & Brown, Inc. announces quarterly cash dividend
feed.summary: Brown & Brown, Inc. (NYSE: BRO) has announced that its board of directors declared a regular quarterly cash dividend of $0.165 per share. The dividend is payable on February 11, 2026, to shareholders of record as of February 4, 2026. Brown & Brown, Inc. is a leading insurance brokerage firm with a global presence, offering comprehensive insurance solutions.

feed.title: What 12 Analyst Ratings Have To Say About Air Products
feed.summary: Twelve analysts have recently provided ratings for Air Products (NYSE: APD), resulting in a wide range of perspectives on the company's stock. The average 12-month price target for Air Products is $283.08, a decrease of 10.96% from the previous average. Despite the varying ratings, financial analysis also highlights that Air Products, while a significant global industrial gas supplier, faced challenges with a decline in revenue and lower profitability metrics compared to industry averages.

feed.title: Geisha Williams elected to PSEG board of directors
feed.summary: Public Service Enterprise Group Incorporated (PSEG) has announced the election of Geisha J. Williams to its Board of Directors, effective March 1, 2026. Williams brings over 35 years of energy industry experience, including her previous role as CEO and president of PG&E Corporation. This appointment follows recent positive financial news for PSEG, including exceeding analyst expectations for Q3 2025 earnings and positive rating upgrades from Wells Fargo and Jefferies.

feed.title: ONEOK raises quarterly dividend by 4% to $1.07 per share
feed.summary: ONEOK, a midstream energy operator, announced a 4% increase in its quarterly dividend to $1.07 per share, making the annualized dividend $4.28 per share. This dividend is payable on February 13, 2026, to shareholders of record as of February 2, 2026. The company, which is a component of the S&P 500, also received recent analyst evaluations, with UBS reiterating a Buy rating and Jefferies initiating coverage with a Hold rating.

feed.title: Kimco Realty Appoints David Jamieson to its Board of Directors
feed.summary: Kimco Realty (NYSE: KIM) has announced the appointment of David Jamieson, their Executive Vice President and Chief Operating Officer, to its Board of Directors, expanding the board to ten members. Jamieson's term began on January 19, 2026. The company leadership, including Chairman Richard B. Saltzman and CEO Conor Flynn, expressed confidence that Jamieson's expertise in operations, technology, and strategic vision will significantly contribute to Kimco's future success and shareholder value.

feed.title: CNO Financial Group Announces Senior Leadership Promotions
feed.summary: CNO Financial Group, Inc. announced the promotions of Steve Janoson to Senior Vice President, Direct and Independent Distribution, and Rachel Spehler to Senior Vice President, Deputy General Counsel and Secretary, effective immediately. Janoson's leadership has been crucial for sustained growth in direct and independent channels, while Spehler has demonstrated outstanding expertise in legal strategy and corporate governance. These promotions aim to strengthen CNO's leadership team and continue its success in serving middle-income America.

feed.title: Oneok (OKE) Boosts Quarterly Dividend by 4%
feed.summary: ONEOK Inc. (OKE) has increased its quarterly dividend by 4% to $1.07 per share, resulting in an annualized dividend of $4.28. The midstream service provider in the energy sector, with a market capitalization of $47.71 billion, announced that shareholders of record by February 2 will receive the payment on February 13. Despite some financial considerations like an Altman Z-Score in the distress zone, the company shows profitability and strong institutional ownership, with its valuation ratios suggesting potential undervaluation.

feed.title: Raymond James raises Charles Schwab stock price target to $121 on growth outlook
feed.summary: Raymond James has increased its price target for Charles Schwab Corp. (NYSE:SCHW) to $121 from $114, maintaining an Outperform rating. This adjustment comes after Schwab's Q4 2025 results, which, despite modestly missing expectations, showed healthy core client metrics and positive underlying financial trends. The firm expects Schwab's focus to shift towards reinvesting maturing investment securities at higher yields in 2026, further bolstered by strong capital generation from Schwab Bank.

feed.title: Why Is Riot Stock Up 40%?
feed.summary: Riot Platforms (RIOT) stock has surged around 40% recently, driven by significant financial improvements in its core Bitcoin mining business and a strategic pivot towards building large-scale data centers for AI and high-performance computing. The company reported record revenue and net income in Q3 2025, along with a strong Bitcoin treasury, while also expanding its growth story beyond crypto by leveraging its power portfolio in Texas. Riot's future trajectory will depend on continued execution in the data center market and the broader Bitcoin market conditions.

feed.title: Invitation Homes acquires build-to-rent developer ResiBuilt
feed.summary: Invitation Homes Inc. has acquired the Atlanta-based build-to-rent developer ResiBuilt for $89 million, with potential for up to $7.5 million in additional payments. ResiBuilt, which has delivered over 4,200 homes since 2018, will retain its current 70 associates and president, Jay Byce, integrating their operations with Invitation Homes to expand their reach in the single-family rental market.

feed.title: Knowmadics Named in the 2025 Gartner® Emerging Tech: Top-funded Startups for Cyber Electronic Defense (CED) Research (December, 2025)
feed.summary: Knowmadics, a provider of electronic warfare and vertical software solutions for government and commercial clients, has been recognized in Gartner's 2025 Emerging Tech report on top-funded startups for Cyber Electronic Defense (CED). The report highlights areas such as drone detection and mitigation, specialist electronic warfare, and wireless airspace cybersecurity. Knowmadics emphasizes the convergence of cyber and electronic warfare at the RF layer and its focus on software-centric capabilities for contested, spectrum-dependent environments.

feed.title: The Travelers Companies, Inc. Announces Quarterly Dividend of $1.10 (NYSE:TRV)
feed.summary: The Travelers Companies, Inc. (NYSE:TRV) has declared a quarterly dividend of $1.10 per share, payable to investors of record on March 10th. The insurance provider has consistently increased its dividend for 21 consecutive years, with analysts expecting continued coverage by future earnings. Shares of TRV traded up $3.09 on Wednesday, following a strong quarterly earnings report that surpassed consensus estimates.

feed.title: Sigma Lithium (NASDAQ:SGML) Trading Down 7.4% - Here's What Happened
feed.summary: Sigma Lithium (NASDAQ:SGML) saw its share price drop by 7.4% during Wednesday's mid-day trading. The stock was trading at $12.47 with a significantly reduced trading volume compared to its daily average. The decline follows recent analyst rating adjustments, including a downgrade from "buy" to "hold" by Canaccord Genuity Group, and comes amidst fluctuating institutional investor activity.

feed.title: Merck unit partners with global health coalition to develop affordable Ebola vaccine
feed.summary: Merck's unit, MSD, and the Coalition for Epidemic Preparedness Innovations (CEPI) have launched a $30 million program to make an updated version of Merck's Ebola vaccine, Ervebo, more affordable and accessible for low- and middle-income countries. This collaboration aims to improve the manufacturing process to increase yield, extend shelf life, reduce costs, and allow for standard refrigeration storage. Hilleman Laboratories will lead the clinical development, while SK bioscience and IDT Biologika handle the drug-substance process and product development.

feed.title: Synovus announces earnings for the fourth quarter 2025
feed.summary: Synovus Financial Corp. reported its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year ended December 31, 2025. The company announced diluted earnings per share of $1.22 for 4Q25, with adjusted diluted EPS at $1.45. This report precedes its merger with Pinnacle Financial Partners, which closed on January 1, 2026, and highlights strong performance contributing to the combined entity's future growth plans.

feed.title: Equifax Announces Earnings Release Date and Conference Call for Fourth Quarter 2025 Results
feed.summary: Equifax (NYSE: EFX) will release its fourth-quarter 2025 financial results on Wednesday, February 4, at 6:30 a.m. ET. Senior management will host a conference call at 8:30 a.m. ET on the same day to discuss the results. Related presentation materials will be available on their investor relations website.

feed.title: Procter & Gamble Earnings Are Out Thursday. Weak Consumer Spending Is a Big Worry.
feed.summary: Procter & Gamble's upcoming earnings report on Thursday is facing investor concern due to weak consumer spending impacting its beauty and healthcare segments. The stock has underperformed recently, leading shareholders to hope the report can alleviate worries about demand for consumer goods.

feed.title: STERIS to Host a Conference Call for Fiscal 2026 Third Quarter Results on February 5, 2026
feed.summary: STERIS plc announced it will host a conference call on February 5, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. ET to discuss its fiscal 2026 third-quarter results. A press release with financial details will be issued on February 4, 2026, after the U.S. market closes. A replay of the call will be available online and via phone starting at 12:00 p.m. ET on February 5, 2026.

feed.title: Johnson & Johnson will continue innovating for better patient healthcare, CEO says
feed.summary: Joseph Wolk, Johnson & Johnson's executive vice president and CEO, affirmed the company's commitment to continuous innovation to improve patient healthcare. He also discussed the company's performance and President Donald Trump's healthcare agenda during an appearance on 'The Claman Countdown'.

feed.title: Raymond James Financial Announces Fiscal Year 2026 Quarterly Earnings Schedule
feed.summary: Raymond James Financial, Inc. has announced its schedule for releasing fiscal year 2026 quarterly earnings. Results will be released at approximately 4:15 p.m. Eastern time, followed by a live conference call at 5:00 p.m. Eastern time on specific dates for each quarter. The company, a diversified financial services provider with $1.77 trillion in client assets, will make related materials available on its investor relations website.

feed.title: CVS Health May Have Violated Antitrust Laws, Republican Lawmakers Say
feed.summary: Republican lawmakers have accused CVS Health Corp. of potentially violating antitrust laws by hindering independent pharmacies from collaborating with third-party competitors. This accusation comes amidst growing bipartisan criticism of health conglomerates like CVS, which integrate insurance, retail pharmacies, and drug benefit management. CVS executives, among others, are slated to testify before a congressional hearing on Thursday regarding these concerns.

feed.title: Honeywell and Flexjet settle litigation, renew engine maintenance deal
feed.summary: Honeywell and Flexjet have resolved their ongoing litigation and extended their aircraft engine maintenance partnership through 2035. The agreement includes related litigation with StandardAero and Duncan Aviation, with the extended maintenance contract serving as partial consideration for the settlement. This development follows other significant Honeywell news, including plans for an IPO of its quantum computing unit, upgrades from JPMorgan and Evercore ISI, and a substantial contract modification from the U.S. Department of War.

feed.title: ProShares UltraPro QQQ ETF Options Spot-On: On January 21st, 392.32K Contracts Were Traded, With 1.21 Million Open Interest
feed.summary: On January 21st, the ProShares UltraPro QQQ ETF (TQQQ.US) saw significant options trading, with a total volume of 392.32K contracts. Call options dominated, accounting for 65.93% of transactions, while open interest for the ETF reached 1.21 million contracts, representing 74.01% of the 30-day average. A notable trade involved 3,527 call options with a strike price of $54.00 expiring on January 23rd, 2026, generating $38,797 in turnover.

feed.title: Should You Buy CVS Health Stock Before Feb. 10?
feed.summary: CVS Health experienced a significant bounce-back in 2025 after a poor performance in 2024, with its share price soaring by 77%. The company is set to report its Q4 earnings on February 10, which will be an early indicator for its 2026 performance. Despite its recent rally, CVS Health stock still appears to be modestly valued with a forward P/E of 11, and analysts suggest that investors may not need to rush to buy shares before the earnings call as major price swings are uncommon after its reports.

feed.title: What Costco Needs to Prove in 2026
feed.summary: In 2026, Costco faces the challenge of justifying its premium valuation by proving its ability to sustain strong membership growth and renewal rates without constant fee increases. The company must also demonstrate profitable international expansion, particularly in newer markets like China, ensuring new stores lead to high-quality, high-renewal memberships. Ultimately, maintaining steady operating leverage and profit growth will be crucial to satisfying elevated investor expectations and reinforcing its long-term growth story.

feed.title: Teledyne (NYSE: TDY) updates board, boosts 2026 performance-tied pay
feed.summary: Teledyne Technologies (NYSE: TDY) has announced several changes to its board and executive compensation structure. Director Kenneth C. Dahlberg will retire in 2026, reducing the board size to 10. The company also detailed 2025 cash bonuses and 2023-2025 performance plan payouts, and set new, performance-based incentive structures for 2026, tying executive pay more closely to operating profit, revenue, and total shareholder return against the S&P 500. Additionally, Executive Chairman Robert Mehrabian received a one-time $3.3 million performance-based restricted stock unit grant.

feed.title: Paysign, Inc. to Present at the Oppenheimer 11th Annual Emerging Growth Conference
feed.summary: Paysign, Inc. (NASDAQ: PAYS) announced its participation in the Oppenheimer 11th Annual Emerging Growth Conference, which will be held virtually on February 3-4, 2026. Paysign's CFO, Jeff Baker, will host one-on-one meetings on February 3rd. The conference focuses on emerging technology, industrial growth, and consumer sectors, providing an opportunity for investors to engage with Paysign, a company specializing in fintech and healthcare payment solutions.

feed.title: Intuitive Surgical continues losses for seven straight sessions
feed.summary: The article states that Intuitive Surgical (ISRG) has experienced a losing streak of seven consecutive sessions. No other details are provided in the content.

feed.title: AGNC INVESTMENT CORP DEP SHS REPSTG 1/1000TH PFD SER G (AGNCL.US) is scheduled to release its earnings report after the market close on January 26.
feed.summary: AGNC Investment Corp (AGNCL.US) is set to release its earnings report after the market close on January 26. The article notes its previous revenue for Q3 2025 was USD 8.36 billion with a net profit of USD 8.06 billion, and Q4 2024 revenue was USD 1.54 billion with a net profit of USD 1.22 billion. Investors are advised to stay tuned for the announcement and subsequent earnings call where management will discuss financial data and operational performance.

feed.title: U.S. home prices in 2025 rose—but buying actually got easier
feed.summary: A new report from Homes.com reveals that while nationwide median home prices increased by 1.1% in 2025, homeownership became more affordable. This improvement is attributed to higher income growth and lower mortgage interest rates, marking the first year-over-year increase in affordability since 2020. Cities in the Midwest saw the highest appreciation, while some Sun Belt cities experienced declines.

feed.title: Charles River to reveal 2026 guidance in Feb. 18 results call
feed.summary: Charles River Laboratories International, Inc. (NYSE: CRL) will release its fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 financial results and provide 2026 guidance on Wednesday, February 18th, before the market opens. A conference call is scheduled for the same day at 8:30 a.m. ET to discuss this information. Investors can access a live webcast and replay of the call through the Investor Relations section of the Company's website.

feed.title: CF Industries Holdings, Inc. Announces Planned Schedule for Quarterly Financial Results to be Released in 2026
feed.summary: CF Industries Holdings, Inc. (NYSE: CF) has announced its planned schedule for releasing quarterly financial results and holding corresponding conference calls in 2026. The company will release its Fourth Quarter 2025 results in February 2026, and its First, Second, and Third Quarter 2026 results in May, August, and November 2026, respectively. Dial-in details and webcast access for these calls will be made available on the Investor Relations section of the company's website.

feed.title: SiTime Appoints AI-Focused Director to Expanded Board
feed.summary: SiTime Corporation (SITM) has expanded its Board of Directors by appointing semiconductor veteran Faraj Aalaei as a Class II director. This strategic move aims to strengthen SiTime's focus on AI and data center segments, given Aalaei's extensive background in AI, networking, and communications. The appointment is seen as positive, reinforcing SiTime's position as a key timing solutions provider for rapidly growing AI workloads, while Spark's AI Analyst maintains a "Neutral" rating on SITM due to mixed financial signals.

feed.title: PROSPERITY BANCSHARES, INC.® ANNOUNCES COMMON STOCK DIVIDEND
feed.summary: Prosperity Bancshares, Inc. (NYSE: PB) announced that its Board of Directors declared a quarterly common stock dividend of $0.60 per share for the first quarter of 2026. This dividend is payable on April 1, 2026, to shareholders of record as of March 13, 2026. The company, a $38.330 billion regional financial holding company as of September 30, 2025, provides banking and investment services across Texas and Oklahoma.
